studyName: Malnutrition and Enteric Disease Network (Mal-ED) Case-Control Study in Brazil

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports for each subject their study cohort, age at enrollment in days, enrollment month, enrollment year, case or control status for diarrhea, sex, age in days at stool sample collection, breast feeding status at sample collection, fecal myeloperoxidase measure, total number of diarrhea episodes during follow-up, total number of follow-up days, total time family has spent in their house, number of rooms in house, the kind of toilet facility in house, the material composing house floor, average household monthly income, and whether family has: a sofa; a cupboard; a table, an electric fan; a radio or transistor; a computer; a refrigerator; a watch or clock; a bicycle; or whether any family member has a bank account.

variables.name: AGED
variables.description: Age in days at stool sample collection

variables.name: BFSTATD
variables.description: Breast feeding status at sample collection

variables.name: CASE
variables.description: Case or control for low birthweight (weight for age Z score)

variables.name: COHORT
variables.description: Study cohort

variables.name: EMON
variables.description: Enrollment month

variables.name: ENAGED
variables.description: Age at enrollment in days

variables.name: ENYEAR
variables.description: Enrollment year

variables.name: FSEABANK
variables.description: Does any member of your household own a bank account?

variables.name: FSEABIC
variables.description: Does your household have a bicycle?

variables.name: FSEACOM
variables.description: Does your household have a computer?

variables.name: FSEACUPB
variables.description: Does your household have a cupboard? Includes cupboards with shutters and open shelves.

variables.name: FSEAFAN
variables.description: Does your household have an electric fan?

variables.name: FSEARAD
variables.description: Does your household have a radio or transistor?

variables.name: FSEAREF
variables.description: Does your household have a refrigerator?

variables.name: FSEASOFA
variables.description: Does your household have a sofa?

variables.name: FSEATAB
variables.description: Does your household have a table?

variables.name: FSEAWCLO
variables.description: Does your household have a watch or clock?

variables.name: FSEFLOOR
variables.description: Main material of the floor (observation)

variables.name: FSEHOUSE
variables.description: How long has your family lived in this house?

variables.name: FSEINCOME
variables.description: What is the average monthly income (reals) for the entire household?

variables.name: FSEROOM1
variables.description: How many rooms are there in your house?

variables.name: FSETOILET
variables.description: What kind of toilet facility do members of your household usually use?

variables.name: FUDURD
variables.description: Duration of follow-up in days

variables.name: MPO
variables.description: Fecal Myeloperoxidase (MPO)  (ng/mL) closest to enrollment

variables.name: NDIR
variables.description: Total number of diarrhea episodes during follow-up

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Gender of infant

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

tables.description: This sample attributes data table reports each sample's body site of origin, analyte type, and tumor status.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified sample ID

tables.description: This subject consent data table contains subject IDs, consent information, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Gender of infant

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
